text:
There once was a wise and kind old owl who lived high in a cozy oak tree. Every morning she would whistle a cheerful tune while munching on delicious acorns. One day, she heard a little chick babbling in fear.

It seemed a fox had cunningly frightened away the owl's friends. Hoping to restore friendship, the wise owl ventured out. The fox grinned wickedly. "Ah, you old fool! You'll never catch me." How wrong he was.

The owl's keen eyesight caught a glimmer in the moonlight. She swooped down, capturing the fox, and sent him scattering.

With apologies, the owl returned to feathered comrades. Rekindling bonds, they spent evenings singing old ballads.

One night, a mysterious voice whispered secrets of the deepest oceans.

"The owl knows them all," it whispered.

The wise owl smiled knowingly. "And I know who speaks."

With those thoughts, she glided away, leaving a tiny drop of tears
